channels.name: All
channels.profile: You're clearly drawn to late-50s/early-60s cool jazz that's calm, harmonically rich, and introspective. You prefer piano trios and lyrical melodies over driving intensity, and you respond to sparse arrangements with emotional depth—Bill Evans seems to be a touchstone for your taste.

channels.profile: You're navigating the intimate, harmonically sophisticated corners of 1950s-60s cool jazz, favoring piano trios and lyrical horn melodies with clear emotional resonance. Your exploration avoids both pop crossover and avant-garde abstraction, staying firmly in refined, chamber-like territory.

channels.profile: You're exploring lyrical, emotionally transparent chamber music, especially from the Romantic era, with a clear preference for warmth and flow over agitation or stark modernism. Schubert's conversational trios resonate, while high-dissonance 20th-century works feel distant.
channels.timePeriod: Classical era and Romantic era and 20th century classical
channels.notes: chamber music, quartets, trios, piano, Glenn Gould, Vladimir Ashkenazy, Mitsuko Uchida, Vladimir Horowitz

channels.profile: You're exploring the soulful, jazzy side of deep house and downtempo—drawn to warm Rhodes, live bass, and organic grooves. You've pulled back from more synthetic or abstract electronic textures, preferring tracks that feel human and melodic.
channels.notes: Deep House, Kaskade, Daft Punk, Andy Caldwell, Blue Six, Llorca, Mark Farina

channels.name: Motown

cardHistory.track: I Heard It Through The Grapevine
Smokestack Lightnin'
What's Going On

channels.profile: You're exploring the soulful, sample-based side of deep house with clear jazz and funk roots. You lean toward moderate energy and organic grooves but pulled back from Floating Points' more abstract, modern electronic sound. Your comfort zone sits between late-90s downtempo and early-2000s melodic house.
channels.notes: Detroit Motown, Howlin' Wolf, Albert King, Muddy Waters

channels.profile: You've disliked polished, message-oriented soul, so we're pivoting to explore raw, instrument-driven energy and deep, personal blues emotion. We'll test your response to powerful vocals, driving rhythms, and iconic guitar work within the 60s-70s US soul and blues canon.
channels.notes: Jay-Z, Tupac Shakur, Nas, Eminem, The Notorious B.I.G., Rakim, and Kendrick Lamar

channels.profile: We're mapping the full breadth of UK & Irish rock for you—from Bowie's grand art-rock anthems to Siouxsie's intense post-punk rhythms and The Psychedelic Furs' introspective new wave. No ratings yet, so we're exploring contrasting energy levels and sonic textures.

channels.name: The Cure

channels.notes: The Psychedelic Furs, Siouxsie and the Banshees

channels.profile: You're exploring the intense, dramatic side of Baroque keyboard music—fiery minor-key sonatas, dense counterpoint, and driving rhythmic energy. You've shown interest in German and Iberian composers while pulling away from lighter French styles, seeking authentic historical performance on harpsichord and organ.

currentCard.reason: A fiery, virtuosic keyboard work by a lesser-known German contemporary of Bach, full of dramatic flourishes and dense counterpoint that matches your taste for intense Baroque energy.

track.name: S. De Brossard Pro pace "ciaccona" G.Dissera Bragadin Contralto, N.Lamon Organo, M.Dalsass Cello.
track.artist: Concerti Santa Maria di Castello, Biancade

queue.reason: A driving, rhythmically intense chaconne by a French composer rarely heard today, offering passionate minor-key drama on harpsichord away from mainstream Baroque repertoire.

track.name: Prelude & Fugue in E Minor "The Wedge" (Scrolling)

queue.reason: A monumental organ work with dramatic intensity and complex counterpoint, fitting your taste for German Baroque intensity while exploring organ territory beyond harpsichord.

track.name: Carlos Seixas' "Keyboard Sonata No. 24" performed by Tengyue "TY" Zhang on a 2020 Matthew Chaffin

queue.reason: Portuguese Baroque composer contemporary with Scarlatti, offering fiery Iberian keyboard writing with driving rhythms and dramatic minor-key intensity.

channels.name: Heavy Metal

cardHistory.track: Orion (Music Video "Original Creation" - 1986) [Full HD/3D]
Wheels Of Confusion

channels.profile: This first batch explores the vast landscape of metal from the US, Canada, and UK/Ireland. You'll hear everything from melodic instrumentals and epic narratives to crushing doom and technical extremity, all while favoring deeper cuts over obvious hits.

currentCard.reason: A galloping, narrative-driven epic with twin guitar harmonies and dramatic vocal delivery, a fan favorite but not a radio single.

queue.reason: A blisteringly fast and technical track with dual-guitar shredding and Rob Halford's soaring vocals, representing speed/power metal's intensity.
A massively slow, down-tuned slab of UK doom metal with oppressive fuzz and nihilistic atmosphere, from an influential cult band.
Technical death metal from a pioneering US band, featuring complex rhythms, philosophical lyrics, and Chuck Schuldiner's distinctive guitar work.
